content.text:
I need to analyze this Bach fugue in A minor carefully. Let me work through the score systematically.

Key observations:
- Key: A minor (no key signature, *a:)
- Time signature: 4/4
- The fugue has 87 bars (bars 80-87 are in the commented-out 5-voice section, but bars 1-79 plus the strophe material around bar 69-70 are the main body)
- Subject enters: bar 1 (soprano), bar 3 (alto), bar 5 (tenor), bar 8 (bass) — typical 4-voice fugue exposition
- The subject is the chromatic descending figure starting on A

Let me trace key regions and cadences through the score:

- Bars 1-8: Exposition in A minor
- Bars 8-13: Continuation, motion toward E minor
- Bars 13-17: E minor region
- Bars 17-22: Return/C major area
- Bars 22-27: Various key regions
- Bars 27-38: Further development
- Around bar 32: B minor
- Around bar 45-50: F# minor / D major area
- Around bar 53-60: Back toward A minor
- Bar 61-69: Stretto/coda region
- Bar 76-79: Final approach
- Bars 80-87: 5-voice conclusion

This is a highly chromatic fugue. Let me identify the structural cadences carefully.

minor"},{"id":"subject_f_sharp","bar_start":33,"bar_end":34,"description":"Subject entry in F# minor (submediant)"},{"id":"episode_4","bar_start":39,"bar_end":48,"description":"Extended episode with sequences, passing through D minor toward A minor"},{"id":"stretto_begin","bar_start":57,"bar_end":63,"description":"Stretto entries begin, close imitation of subject in multiple approaching"},{"id":"coda_5voice","bar_start":80,"bar_end":87,"description":"Five-voice coda with final subject statement and cadential close on A minor"}]}
